title: Complete cross-service global model pool
objective: Complete the cross-service global model pool described by docs/harness/model-pool-design.md so every fixed AI scene has an owned, administrable model configuration; industry-chain supports per-scene hot swap; sparrow-ai resolves its chat and embedding models through a secure internal contract with a bounded local fallback; and the maintained design document records the deployed contract, failure behavior and verification evidence.

in: Shared model scene, kind, configuration and validation contracts in sparrow-common
sparrow-industry-chain model configuration persistence, activation, bootstrap, hot swap and internal read contract
sparrow-ai startup/runtime model resolution for chat, streaming chat and embedding
Administrator model configuration types, form, grouping and activation behavior
Additive idempotent migration, focused tests, maintained design documentation and harness manifest
out: Moving model configuration persistence out of sparrow-industry-chain or sharing its database credentials
Introducing a new business service, shared LangChain4j dependency or distributed transaction
Changing public AI chat, RAG or industry-chain API contracts
Deploying to or mutating an external environment, production secrets or production model configurations
Unrelated user-owned frontend and backend edits already present in the dirty worktree

acceptanceCriteria.description: The model_config schema and shared contracts represent every fixed scene and enforce scene-kind compatibility without adding a cross-service build dependency.
evidence: ModelScene declares six fixed scenes and each scene's expected ModelKind; ModelConfigRules rejects unknown, mismatched and out-of-range configurations.
The additive runtime schema and migrate-model-config-scene.sql retain database ownership in sparrow-industry-chain; both business modules depend only on sparrow-common.

acceptanceCriteria.description: sparrow-industry-chain loads, activates and hot-swaps chat models independently per scene while preserving encrypted keys, masking, audit and legacy defaults.
evidence: ChatModelProvider holds per-scene atomic chat/streaming references and all four industry-chain consumers use explicit scenes.
ModelConfigService validates, encrypts, audits and performs local model swaps only after the activation/update transaction commits.

acceptanceCriteria.description: sparrow-ai obtains only its chat and embedding scene configurations through a protected internal runtime contract and starts predictably when the owner is unavailable by using a bounded environment fallback.
evidence: The /internal/chains/model-configs contract is outside gateway routes, permits only sparrow-ai scenes, requires a 32-character deployment token and never logs the response/key.
ModelPoolConfigResolver tests cover remote preference, kind mismatch, owner failure and missing-token environment fallback; focused sparrow-ai tests passed.

acceptanceCriteria.description: The administrator UI can create, edit, group and activate configurations by fixed scene without exposing API keys or allowing an incompatible model kind.
evidence: ModelConfigView groups all six scenes, derives kind from scene, uses embedding-specific fields/tests, prevents moving active configurations and states the actual activation timing.
The Vite production build passed; in-app browser visual verification was attempted but the browser plugin failed to initialize with Cannot redefine property: process.

acceptanceCriteria.description: Focused backend tests, frontend production build, deterministic guards and change-aware verification pass; the design document matches the implemented ownership, security, fallback and rollout behavior.
evidence: node tools/harness.mjs changed passed both deterministic guards, all seven backend modules with 187 tests and the frontend production build.
docs/harness/model-pool-design.md now records implemented ownership, fixed scenes, schema, token-protected internal contract, activation timing, fallback, rollback, smoke steps and known limitations.
git diff --check passed with line-ending normalization warnings only.

steps.description: Reconcile the existing partial implementation with repository boundaries and establish focused baseline evidence.
steps.verification: Baseline passed: 40 common tests, 56 industry-chain tests, frontend production build and both deterministic guards.

steps.description: Complete the shared contract, persistence, multi-scene provider, bootstrap and administrator UI slice.
steps.verification: Shared contract, strict scene-kind validation, schema, per-scene persistence/provider behavior and grouped UI were implemented; focused tests and frontend build passed.

steps.description: Add the protected internal configuration endpoint and integrate sparrow-ai chat, streaming and embedding model resolution with fallback.
steps.verification: Token-protected internal read contract and sparrow-ai remote-first startup selection with bounded environment fallback were implemented and tested.

steps.description: Add focused contract, failure-path and UI/build coverage and verify the real administrative flow where locally available.
steps.verification: Focused reactor passed 43 common, 30 sparrow-ai and 65 industry-chain tests; frontend production build passed. Browser visual verification was blocked by a plugin initialization fault.

steps.description: Update the maintained design document and task record, then run guards and change-aware verification.
steps.verification: The design/architecture documents and manifest were updated; change-aware validation passed 187 backend tests, frontend build and both guards; git diff --check passed.

decisions: Keep model_config and key encryption in sparrow-industry-chain; sparrow-ai consumes a runtime contract and never connects to the owner database.
Keep ModelScene, ModelKind and data-only validation contracts in sparrow-common; keep LangChain4j model construction service-owned.
Use fixed code-owned scenes and reject scene-kind mismatches instead of accepting arbitrary scene text.
Treat environment configuration as a startup/recovery fallback for sparrow-ai, not as a second administrative source of truth.
Protect the plaintext internal configuration response with an un-routed /internal path plus a deployment-provided token of at least 32 characters; fail closed and fall back when the token is absent.

Keep sparrow-ai configuration as a startup snapshot in this version and state the restart requirement in the administrator UI and maintained design; runtime refresh remains a separately testable evolution.

commands.result: PASS: 43 common, 30 sparrow-ai and 65 sparrow-industry-chain tests.
PASS: Vite production build with the six-scene grouped model configuration UI.
PASS: both guards, all seven backend modules with 187 tests, and frontend production build.
PASS: no whitespace errors; line-ending normalization warnings only.
BLOCKED: browser plugin initialization failed with Cannot redefine property: process; no alternative browser automation was used.

nextAction: Deploy with matching SPARROW_MODEL_POOL_INTERNAL_TOKEN values, run the idempotent migration twice, create/activate all six scenes and execute the authenticated live-model smoke matrix documented in model-pool-design.md.
